title: hypericum quartinianum subsp. roeperianum
intro: Hypericum quartinianum subsp. roeperianum, also known as the St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant species with yellow, star-shaped flowers and a history of traditional medicinal use. It is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of this plant include the potential treatment of anxiety, insomnia, and depression, as well as its wound-healing properties and ability to reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of St. John's Wort are often made into teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
